Title: Exploiting Natural Variation in Tomato to Define Pathway Structure and Metabolic Regulation of Fruit Polyphenolics in the Lycopersicum Complex

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Flavonol-3-O-glucosides such as quercetin-3-glucoside and kaempferol-3-glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the 3-O position of flavonol aglycones to form flavonol-3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for downstream decorated flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolics with health-beneficial antioxidant properties; also implicated in UV, cold, drought tolerance and disease/pest resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel; mature fruit; leaf; stem
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavonol-3-O-glucosides and downstream flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key decoration step; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; overexpression in Arabidopsis; tomato transient overexpression; tomato VIGS suppression; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in mature fruit; conserved active sequence across multiple tomato species
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predicted/observed active in tissues accumulating flavonol-3-O-glucosides; tested in tomato fruit
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in Arabidopsis overexpression; transient overexpression in tomato fruit
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; public RNA-seq from Koenig et al. 2013 and Bolger et al. 2014; TOM2 microarray
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening stage; cross-species comparison; VIGS and transient overexpression conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g083440 was annotated as the tomato flavonoid 3-O-glucosyltransferase in the refined polyphenol pathway. 2. Recombinant protein glucosylated flavonols using UDP-glucose. 3. Overexpression increased flavonol-3Glc-related compounds in Arabidopsis and tomato, whereas VIGS reduced flavonol-3-O-glucosides in tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering and breeding elevated tomato flavonol glycosides by increasing the precursor pool for downstream flavonol decorations; can be stacked with downstream glycosylation/acylation genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study also notes this gene had been annotated as anthocyanin 3-O-glucosyltransferase in purple transgenic tomato; active PSPG motifs were highly conserved across LYCO, PIMP, NEOR, HABR, and PENN.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers apiose to the 2''-O position of the glucose moiety on flavonol-3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate decorated intermediates for tri-/tetra-glycosylated flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolic antioxidants and stress-protective specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes accumulation of flavonol-3Glc2''Api-containing metabolites; lack of expression/absence associated with no accumulation in CHMI and NEOR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cross-species metabolite-pattern inference; phylogenetic analysis of tomato UGTs; in silico expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in HABR leaves according to in silico analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predicted active where flavonol-3Glc2''Api derivatives accumulate; absent/non-expressed in CHMI and NEOR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; public RNA-seq; phylogenetic analysis of all tomato UGTs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Species and tissue differences; fruit ripening stage
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g008860 was assigned as a candidate flavonol-3Glc-2''-O-apiosyltransferase. 2. Its phylogenetic placement and expression pattern were consistent with the apiosylation step. 3. Absence of corresponding metabolites in CHMI and NEOR supported lack of this activity in those species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for diversifying flavonol glycoside structures in tomato breeding and metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assignment is candidate-level and not functionally validated biochemically in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers rhamnose to the 6'' position of glucose on flavonol-3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to major rutin/nicotiflorin-type flavonols and further acylated products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavonol-3Glc6''Rha derivatives; low expression in HABR correlates with low rutin-type metabolites and high accumulation of 3Glc intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cross-species metabolite-pattern inference; in silico expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in PENN and lower in HABR based on in silico expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predicted active in tissues/species accumulating rutin-type flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Species and tissue differences; fruit ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc09g059170 was assigned as the tomato flavonoid-3-O-glc-6''-O-rhamnosyltransferase. 2. Lower expression in HABR and higher expression in PENN matched species differences in 3Glc6''Rha-type flavonols. 3. This step channels flux from 3-O-glucosides toward rutin-like products.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for increasing rutin/nicotiflorin accumulation in tomato fruit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment is based on orthology and metabolite/transcript correlation in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 7-O position of flavonoid substrates to form 7-O-glucosylated flavonols
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms terminal or near-terminal decorated flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolic antioxidants and stress-related specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes accumulation of flavonol-7-O-glucosides; PENN fruits and leaves lacking these metabolites were predicted to lack expression; transient overexpression line showed relatively low Q3Glc6''Rha7Glc change and function remains uncertain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; in silico expression analysis; tomato VIGS and transient overexpression metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predicted in HABR seedlings/leaves from RNA-seq-supported analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Absent or non-expressed in PENN fruits and leaves based on metabolite-guided prediction
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; public RNA-seq; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Species and tissue differences; fruit ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g079350 was proposed as a candidate flavonoid 7-O-glucosyltransferase. 2. Species lacking flavonol-7-O-glucosides were predicted to lack its expression. 3. Experimental transient overexpression/VIGS data were inconclusive, so further characterization is required.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for generating highly decorated flavonol glycosides, but requires stronger functional confirmation before deployment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note the protein does not cluster clearly with known F7GT clades and explicitly state further characterization is required.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose residues via C-glycosidic linkage to phloretin at the 3' and 5' positions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized polyphenolic metabolite; likely contributes to stress-protective chemodiversity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mature fruits of red-fruited species, PERU and HABR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes accumulation of P35diGlc in species/tissues where this metabolite is detected
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog search using characterized citrus/rice/apple genes; phylogenetic analysis; metabolite-pattern association
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Species and tissue differences; fruit ripening
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc02g088500 was assigned as a candidate phloretin-3',5'-C-di-glucosyltransferase. 2. It was identified by orthology to known flavonoid-C-glucosyltransferases and integrated with metabolite evidence. 3. This gene explains the branch leading to P35diGlc in certain tomato species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to introduce or enhance uncommon dihydrochalcone C-glycosides in tomato germplasm
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate assignment only; no direct biochemical validation in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: p-Coumaroyl-CoA reduction to phloretin precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Reductase candidate orthologous to apple CDRB
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced intermediate leading to phloretin / dihydrochalcone formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces p-coumaroyl-CoA in the dihydrochalcone branch, supplying precursor for phloretin-derived compounds
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized polyphenolic metabolite class contributing to chemodiversity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely enables dihydrochalcone branch and accumulation of phloretin-derived metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potential branch-defining step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog search and pathway reconstruction from metabolite structure
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g078740 was assigned as a tomato ortholog of p-coumaroyl-CoA reductase. 2. This annotation supports the reconstructed dihydrochalcone branch. 3. The gene is proposed to provide precursors for phloretin-derived metabolites such as P35diGlc.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering novel dihydrochalcone accumulation in tomato
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct enzymatic validation in this paper.
